target.name: Beyond Aero
target.founder: Valéry Manuel, Eloa Guillotin, Hugo Tarlé

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Beyond Aero is a Series A transportation company founded by Valéry Manuel, Eloa Guillotin, and Hugo Tarl, having raised $44M to date. The valuation analysis benchmarks against a comp universe of 8 private firms in transportation (including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger), with no public peers available in the narrow frontier-transport segment.

Given the limited peer set, only the stage-progression method proved applicable. This approach anchors to the $44M total raised and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $88M. The peer-median method could not be applied due to insufficient comparable data points within the same stage and valuation tier—only 1 same-stage transportation peer existed, falling short of the 3-company minimum threshold for statistical reliability.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $75M (low) to $101M (high), with a median of $88M. This range reflects the step-progression methodology applied to current capital raised. The primary uncertainty stems from the sparse peer universe; frontier-transportation remains a fragmented sector with limited public comparable data and few private firms at analogous stages. Additional validation would require either expanded peer sourcing across adjacent transportation subsectors or forward-looking revenue/margin benchmarks as the company progresses toward Series B.
